topStories.title: Bulls to return after March massacre? Elara sees limited downside for Nifty after 11% crash amid Iran-US war
topStories.description: After an 11% March crash driven by the escalating Iran–US conflict, Elara Securities says the Nifty may have limited downside ahead. Historical data shows conflicts typically cap drawdowns near 10%, and with valuations now 7% below long-term averages, the brokerage expects a potential rebound as risks gradually ease.

topStories.title: Face-Check: Could Barack Obama Be Arrested Over Russia Hoax Claims?
topStories.description: Claims by Trump allies against Barack Obama over the "Russia hoax" have resurfaced, but remain unproven. Past probes confirmed Russian interference in 2016 polls, with no evidence of a criminal...

topStories.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said the modules manufactured in Sanand will reach American companies and from there power the entire world

topStories.description: Raja Raghuvanshi's brother, Vipin Raghuvanshi, reportedly welcomed a baby boy on Sunday. The family hailed it as a divine sign: “God has sent Raja back to us.”

topStories.description: Somali federal government hails Baidoa's transition, urging peace and unity amid political changes and rising tensions.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit $4 a Gallon on Average
dailyNews.description: A month since the first U.S.-Israeli attacks and Iran’s response effectively shut off Persian Gulf oil, drivers are paying significantly more to fill up.

dailyNews.title: Drivers Count Their Pennies As Gas Hits $4 a Gallon
dailyNews.description: Since the Iran conflict began on Feb. 28, gas prices across the United States have increased about 35 percent. They are now above $4 a gallon, and drivers are wincing.

dailyNews.title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
dailyNews.description: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.

dailyNews.title: Iran’s Fractured Leadership Is Struggling to Coordinate, Officials Say
dailyNews.description: As leaders are killed and replaced, Iranian negotiators may not know what their government is willing to concede in any negotiations.

dailyNews.title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
dailyNews.description: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.

dailyNews.title: Offensive vs. Defensive: On Iran, the Distinction Matters for Britain’s Leader
dailyNews.description: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.

dailyNews.title: Deaths in ICE Custody Are Growing, and Trump Defends Ballroom Plans
dailyNews.description: Plus, a three-minute, multimillion-dollar art heist.

dailyNews.title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
dailyNews.description: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.

dailyNews.title: Inside Russia During an Internet Crackdown
dailyNews.description: Our international correspondent Valerie Hopkins walks us through how she connects to the internet in Russia as the Kremlin clamps down and Moscow experiences major internet blackouts.

dailyNews.title: A Cat-and-Mouse Game of Russian Internet Restrictions and Evasion
dailyNews.description: As the Kremlin spends heavily on censorship technology, Russians are scrambling to find new ways to circumvent the limits.

dailyNews.title: ‘The Book of Mormon’ Is Sorry if You Were Offended for 15 Years
dailyNews.description: The taboo-busting, gasp-inducing Broadway musical comedy has been a hit with audiences and critics. But could it be produced today?

topStories.title: For Trump, the Artemis II Moon Mission Offers a Shot to Cement His Legacy
topStories.description: No president since the Apollo era has pushed harder to return to the moon than President Trump. But he wants a space achievement that is about “more than getting rocks this time.”

topStories.title: After Months of Threats, Trump Softens His Stance on Blocking Oil to Cuba
topStories.description: The White House press secretary said the United States would evaluate oil shipments to Cuba on a “case-by-case basis,” after a Russian tanker full of crude reached the island.

topStories.title: Edward Russo Says Donald Trump Is an ‘Environmental Hero’
topStories.description: Edward Russo, the only member of a White House task force, thinks the president doesn’t get enough credit for conservation at his golf courses, among other things.

topStories.title: Trump Reveals New Look for White House Ballroom After Times Article
topStories.description: Architects and preservation organizations have warned that little scrutiny has been given to the project’s design plans.

topStories.title: DeSantis Signs Bill to Rename Florida Airport for Trump
topStories.description: The airport near West Palm Beach will be rebranded the President Donald J. Trump International Airport on July 1, if approved by the Federal Aviation Administration.

topStories.title: Scoop: House Speaker Mike Johnson's allies unleash $10M campaign to spotlight Trump tax cuts
topStories.description: The American Action Network says it's launching a $10 million ad blitz through Tax Day to promote the Working Families Tax Cuts Act across 37 congressional districts.

dailyNews.title: Trump admin official shreds NY Times over multiple corrections to report on Silicon Valley initiative
dailyNews.description: Jacob Helberg detailed the Trump administration’s "Pax Silica" investor consortium after The New York Times issued corrections clarifying funding figures and his remarks.

dailyNews.title: Watch Live: Pete Hegseth, Dan Caine hold news briefing as Iran strikes Kuwaiti oil tanker
dailyNews.description: President Trump on Monday renewed his calls to attack Iranian civilian energy and water infrastructure if no deal is reached to end the war.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court to weigh Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court will consider the legality of President Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens to invade Iran, "obliterate" its energy resources
dailyNews.description: Energy markets remain volatile as President Trump threatens Iran with an invasion to seize its oil while also suggesting a deal could soon end the war.

dailyNews.title: 3/23: Face the Nation
dailyNews.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," National Security Adviser Mike Waltz and Senate Homeland Security chair Rand Paul join as the backlash grows against the Trump administration for the deportation of alleged Venezuelan gang members. Plus. House intelligence committee member Democratic Rep. Jim Himes joins.

dailyNews.title: DeSantis signs bill to rename Palm Beach airport after Trump
dailyNews.description: President Trump's family business filed a trademark application for the name in February in response to the Florida bill.

dailyNews.title: Medicaid cuts threaten hundreds of hospitals, new report finds
dailyNews.description: More than 400 hospitals across the United States are at high risk of closing or cutting services because of the Medicaid cuts in President Donald Trump’s “big, beautiful bill,” according to an analysis from the progressive watchdog group Public Citizen

dailyNews.title: Trump shares renderings of his presidential library — a massive skyscraper in Miami
dailyNews.description: The mocked-up library dwarfs the nearby Freedom Tower, a downtown Miami landmark.

dailyNews.title: WH pressed on Trump’s threat to hit civilian infrastructure in Iran
dailyNews.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Donald Trump threatened the destruction of Iran's civilian energy and water infrastructure if a deal to end the war and reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz is not reached soon. NBC News' Garrett Haake questioned White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt about the president's threat.

dailyNews.title: Trump lashes out at UK and France, telling allies 'the U.S.A. won't be there to help you anymore'
dailyNews.description: Trump has again expressed his disdain for European allies that have refused to take part in the U.S. and Israel's military operation against Iran.

dailyNews.title: Army investigates helicopter flyby at Kid Rock's Nashville home
dailyNews.description: Kid Rock is a leading supporter of President Donald Trump, the subject of "No Kings" demonstrations around the U.S. on the day the helicopters showed up.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Israel and Gulf states report new Iranian strikes, tanker hit
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.
